weeks.weekly_summary: Establish aerobic foundation with 2 easy runs on preferred days (Monday, Wednesday). Low volume given recent 1-hour weeks. Build consistency and assess fitness baseline.
weeks.coaching_notes: Athlete has very low recent training volume (9.9 km over 14 days). Week 1 is conservative to rebuild base safely. No hard sessions—focus on easy conversational pace and habit formation. All runs at Zone 2 (65-75% max HR, ~6:15-6:45 min/km pace based on recent 6:10 average). Rest days allow recovery before progressive loading.
recovery_flags: Very low training volume baseline—gradual progression critical to avoid injury
No HR data available—use perceived effort and pace guidance
Monitor for excessive fatigue despite low mileage (detraining effect)
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 2 adds a tempo component to Wednesday run; Monday remains easy. Total volume increases to ~22 km as aerobic base solidifies.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest or light mobility/stretching.

days.title: Easy 7 km
days.description: Relaxed aerobic run at conversational pace. Focus on steady rhythm and breathing. No pressure—this is about building consistency.
days.notes: Pace: ~6:20–6:50 min/km. If felt sluggish, drop to 6.5 km.

days.title: Pilates Session
days.description: Light pilates for core strength and flexibility. Complements running without creating interference.
days.notes: Light impact—no fatigue carryover to Wednesday run.

days.title: Easy 8 km
days.description: Slightly longer easy run. Maintain conversational pace throughout. Build volume gradually.
days.notes: Pace: ~6:20–6:50 min/km. Feel free to walk 1–2 min if needed.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest or light yoga/mobility.

days.title: Gym Session
days.description: Strength and conditioning: focus on lower body, core, and posterior chain. Avoid excessive fatigue—keep it controlled.
days.notes: Light-to-moderate weights. No heavy squats that could impact Monday run.

days.title: Football Match
days.description: Recreational football. Good for sport-specific fitness and mental freshness. Manage intensity—focus on technique, not max effort.
days.notes: Don't overextend—remember you have 2 quality run days. Hydrate well.

weeks.weekly_summary: Introduce tempo work on Wednesday while maintaining easy Monday run. Volume increases to ~22 km. Quality session (tempo) builds lactate threshold capacity for half-marathon pace preparation.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 2 begins polarization: Monday stays easy (Zone 2, 7.5 km), Wednesday includes 4 km at tempo (Zone 3, ~5:50–6:00 min/km) sandwiched between easy warm-up and cool-down. Half-marathon pace (~5:50/km for 2:00 target) is slightly faster than pure tempo, so tempo work conditions the system. Total weekly time ~4.0 hours. Cross-training unchanged. Volume increase is steady (10% jump)—monitor fatigue carefully.
recovery_flags: First introduction of tempo—pace conservatively, emphasize control
If Wednesday tempo feels too hard (HR spiking, breathing ragged), reduce tempo distance to 3 km
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 3 maintains 2 sessions/week. Wednesday tempo distance increases to 5 km; Monday stays easy. Consider adding a shorter Sunday run if athlete tolerates load.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest or very light mobility (yoga, stretching).

days.title: Easy 7.5 km
days.description: Relaxed aerobic run. Slightly longer than Week 1 Monday to build endurance base. Conversational pace.
days.notes: Pace: ~6:20–6:50 min/km.

days.title: Pilates Session
days.description: Core and flexibility work. Prepares body for tempo effort on Wednesday.

days.title: Tempo Run: 2 km easy + 4 km @ tempo + 1 km easy
days.description: Warm up 2 km easy. Run middle 4 km at comfortably hard tempo pace (lactate threshold). Cool down 1 km easy. Focus on controlled breathing and steady effort—not a race.
days.notes: Tempo pace: ~5:50–6:00 min/km (slightly faster than easy, but not gasping). If overly hard, back off to 3 km tempo next week.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest or light mobility.
days.notes: Important day after tempo—allow full recovery.

days.title: Gym Session
days.description: Strength: lower body, core, glutes. Keep intensity controlled—complement running, don't compete.
days.notes: Avoid heavy squats day after tempo.

days.title: Football Match
days.description: Recreational football. Maintain sustainable intensity—not a time trial.
days.notes: Good recovery day after tempo fatigue.

weeks.weekly_summary: Build on tempo fitness. Wednesday tempo increases to 5 km. Monday easy remains foundational. Introduce Sunday easy run to reach 3 sessions/week within 4-hour budget. Total distance ~27 km.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 3 adds a third run (Sunday easy, 5 km) while maintaining 2 quality sessions (Monday easy 7.5 km, Wednesday 2 km + 5 km tempo + 1 km = 8 km). Total time stays within 4 hours (~245 min running + cross-training). Tempo intensity maintained at Zone 3. This week tests whether athlete can sustain 3-run structure comfortably. If fatigue signals emerge (persistent elevated HR, soreness, mood changes), drop Sunday run and revert to 2 runs/week for Week 4.
recovery_flags: Adding third run—monitor total load (should be ~27 km, ~245 min running)
If cumulative fatigue evident, drop Sunday run and keep 2 runs/week for final block
Tempo is now core quality session—prioritize good execution over volume
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 4 (final) begins taper. Reduce volume by 15–20%. Wednesday tempo drops to 3 km (shorter, sharper). Monday easy holds. Sunday drops or becomes very short (3–4 km recovery run). Prepare for race week mentality.

days.title: Easy 5 km
days.description: Short, relaxed run to add volume without stress. Perfect complement to harder Wednesday session.
days.notes: Pace: ~6:40–7:10 min/km. Very conversational—should feel fresh.

days.title: Easy 7.5 km
days.description: Steady aerobic run. Maintain consistency with Week 2. No fatigue from Sunday—Monday should feel strong.
days.notes: Pace: ~6:20–6:50 min/km.

days.title: Pilates Session
days.description: Core and mobility work.

days.title: Tempo Run: 2 km easy + 5 km @ tempo + 1 km easy
days.description: Warm up 2 km easy. Run middle 5 km at tempo pace (lactate threshold). Cool down 1 km easy. Slightly longer tempo segment than Week 2—demonstrates improving capacity.
days.notes: Tempo pace: ~5:50–6:00 min/km. If feeling strong, maintain pace. If labored, don't push—priority is consistency over speed.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest or light mobility.

days.title: Gym Session
days.description: Strength and conditioning: lower body, core, glutes. Lighter session this week to manage cumulative fatigue.
days.notes: Keep intensity moderate—not a heavy lifting day.

days.title: Football Match
days.description: Recreational football. Good for variety and mental freshness.

weeks.weekly_summary: Begin taper (15% volume reduction from Week 3). Maintain intensity but reduce duration. Wednesday tempo becomes shorter, sharper interval work (3 × 1 km @ tempo). Monday easy at 6 km. Total distance ~18 km over 4 days. Shift focus to recovery, sleep, nutrition, and race logistics.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 4 is critical taper before race (race is 11 weeks post-block based on 15 weeks total from plan start). Volume drops 33% (from 27 km to 18 km). Intensity maintained—Wednesday features 3 × 1 km repeats at tempo pace with 1 min easy jog between reps. This 'strides' mentality keeps neuromuscular sharpness without accumulating fatigue. Monday easy stays low-key. No Sunday run this week—complete rest before race. Pilates, gym, and football continue but at lower intensity. Last 3–4 days: minimal running, focus on hydration, sleep, carb-loading.
recovery_flags: Taper will feel 'easy'—normal nervousness. Trust the training.
If agitation emerges, do light 20–30 min jog to settle mind.
Sleep, hydration, nutrition are now PRIMARY. Not training time.
weeks.next_week_preview: Race week (Week 5+): Not included in this 4-week block, but typical race week = 2–3 easy shakeout runs (20–30 min) + 1 × 4–6 strides on Friday/Saturday. Race on Sunday.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest. Focus on meal prep, hydration, and mental preparation for race.
days.notes: Sleep 8+ hours. Hydrate consistently.

days.title: Easy 6 km
days.description: Very light aerobic run. Short and comfortable—maintain fitness without fatigue.
days.notes: Pace: ~6:40–7:10 min/km. Should feel easy and strong.

days.title: Light Pilates
days.description: Very light stretching and core activation. Mobility focus, zero fatigue.
days.notes: Shorter session. Emphasis on breathing and ease.

days.title: Tempo Intervals: 1.5 km easy + 3 × 1 km @ tempo (1 min easy jog between) + 1 km easy
days.description: Warm up 1.5 km easy. Run 3 repeats of 1 km at tempo pace (not a sprint—controlled effort) with 1 min easy jog recovery between. Cool down 1 km easy. Total 4 × 1 km threshold work. Maintains sharpness without excessive fatigue.
days.notes: 1 km pace: ~5:45–5:55 min/km (slightly faster than marathon pace to practice digging in). Rest 1 min jog between. This is NOT an interval session—controlled tempo, not max effort.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest. Hydration and meal prep focus.

days.title: Light Gym or Mobility
days.description: Optional light stretching, foam rolling, or mobility. No heavy lifting.
days.notes: Skip if not feeling recovered. Prioritize rest.

days.title: Race Week Rest
days.description: No football. Complete rest day. Manage race nerves with visualization, hydration, and sleep.
days.notes: If anxious, light 15 min walk allowed. Stay hydrated. Prepare race kit.

coaching_overview: This 4-week block is designed for a half-marathon racer targeting 2:00 (5:50/km pace) with a 4-hour weekly time budget and only 2 preferred running days (Monday, Wednesday). **Week 1** rebuilds aerobic base from very low training volume (9.9 km over 14 days) with 2 easy runs (7 + 8 km). **Week 2** introduces tempo work (4 km @ Zone 3) on Wednesday while Monday stays easy, establishing polarization (80% easy, 20% hard). **Week 3** adds a third run (Sunday easy) and increases Wednesday tempo to 5 km, reaching ~27 km total. **Week 4** begins taper with 33% volume reduction; Wednesday becomes 3 × 1 km tempo repeats (sharper, shorter) to maintain race-readiness while allowing recovery. Cross-training (Football/Gym/Pilates) is embedded throughout to fill non-running days and support running fitness without interference. **Key principles:** (1) Strict adherence to Monday–Wednesday running schedule, respecting athlete's time availability; (2) Conservative Week 1–2 progression due to low baseline fitness; (3) Tempo work replaces 'junk mileage,' building lactate threshold for half-marathon pace; (4) Taper philosophy: reduce volume 15–33%, preserve intensity to keep neuromuscular sharpness; (5) No HR/VO2 data available—all pacing is effort-based (conversational, comfortably hard, controlled). **Total block distance: ~85 km over 4 weeks (~240 min running time).** Post-block: race week (not included) would feature 2–3 shakeout runs and race on Week 5 (15 weeks from plan start = race day).
weekly_summary: Establish aerobic foundation with 2 easy runs on preferred days (Monday, Wednesday). Low volume given recent 1-hour weeks. Build consistency and assess fitness baseline.
coaching_notes: Athlete has very low recent training volume (9.9 km over 14 days). Week 1 is conservative to rebuild base safely. No hard sessions—focus on easy conversational pace and habit formation. All runs at Zone 2 (65-75% max HR, ~6:15-6:45 min/km pace based on recent 6:10 average). Rest days allow recovery before progressive loading.
recovery_flags: Very low training volume baseline—gradual progression critical to avoid injury
No HR data available—use perceived effort and pace guidance
Monitor for excessive fatigue despite low mileage (detraining effect)
